### Post by videocheez on 2007-06-24
I cannot see my FAT 32 drive USB external hardrive.  I would like to know a command to make it show up in my computer.  Please review my ftsab file and the output from the following code (please note that sde1 is the drive that I cant see):

```

sudo fdisk  -l

```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0

/dev/sdh1 /media/DRIVE_O ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
/dev/sde1 /media/HD-HCU2 vfat defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0

### Post by BobCFC on 2007-06-24
My external USB Western Digital My Book Pro just automounts because of USB hotplug.  It does not have an entry in my fstab.  I see your file was autogenerated... have you tried commenting the line it out with a # maybe?

---

### Post by videocheez on 2007-06-25
> **BobCFC said:**
> My external USB Western Digital My Book Pro just automounts because of USB hotplug.  It does not have an entry in my fstab.  I see your file was autogenerated... have you tried commenting the line it out with a # maybe?

Great advice my friend.  :D I don't think fstab liked this line of code:
/dev/sde1 /media/HD-HCU2 vfat defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
  
I typed
```

dmesg | tail

```

and the error log said that this part en_US.UTF-8 0 0 doesn't look good.
This is my only FAT32 external drive.  My other three externals are NTFS, where I believe that lass part of the code does apply.

Anyways,  Thanks again,

VC

---
